reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C-O and C-C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N-O bond rearrangement in nitrile oxide generation; loss of H2O from ethyl nitroacetate

reagents: ethyl nitroacetate (2.0 equiv) in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES
feed_streams.reasoning: Both substrates are premixed in DES; nitrile oxide must be generated in presence of dipolarophile to avoid dimerization. No incompatibility between substrates at room temperature — reaction only proceeds at elevated temperature.

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ via thermal dehydration; 2 equiv used to drive reaction to completion

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent system; acts as reaction medium and may promote nitrile oxide formation

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent system

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) in situ. The nitro group undergoes tautomerization to the aci-nitro form followed by elimination of water.

Regioselective 1,3-dipolar [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide (C≡N⁺-O⁻) with phenylacetylene. The oxygen of the nitrile oxide attacks the terminal carbon of the alkyne, and the carbon of the nitrile oxide bonds to the internal carbon, giving the 3,5-disubstituted isoxazole regioselectively.
Aromatization of the initial 4,5-dihydroisoxazole cycloadduct to the fully aromatic isoxazole product (spontaneous under reaction conditions with alkyne dipolarophile — product is already aromatic).

stream_logic.reasoning: Single premixed feed stream. Both substrates must be present together when heated so the transient nitrile oxide intermediate is immediately trapped by phenylacetylene, preventing nitrile oxide dimerization to furoxan. No catalyst or base needed. DES is viscous (~50 cP at RT) so the feed should be warmed slightly (~40-50°C) to reduce viscosity for pumping. Concentration: phenylacetylene 0.5 M, ethyl nitroacetate 1.0 M in 1 mL DES.

mixing_order_reasoning: Only one feed stream is needed. Both substrates are dissolved in the DES at room temperature where no reaction occurs. The reaction is initiated purely by heating to 120°C in the reactor coil. Premixing ensures the dipolarophile (phenylacetylene) is present to trap the nitrile oxide as soon as it forms, suppressing the competitive dimerization pathway.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species or radicals involved. The reaction proceeds via a concerted pericyclic mechanism under air.
quench_reasoning: No quench needed inline. The product is stable at room temperature. Post-reaction workup (water addition, DCM extraction) is an offline purification step, not a chemical quench.
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction. No photocatalyst or photosensitizer is involved. Light is not required.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism and flow translation. Key uncertainties: (1) DES viscosity at room temperature is high (~50-100 cP for TBAB/EG 1:5), which will affect pumping and pressure drop — may need to warm the feed reservoir to 40-50°C or use a syringe pump capable of handling viscous fluids. (2) Water is generated as a byproduct during nitrile oxide formation; in a sealed flow system under back-pressure this should not be problematic. (3) The 15 min residence time at 120°C is very amenable to flow; a back-pressure regulator (BPR, ~5-10 bar) should be used to prevent solvent boiling (EG bp 197°C, but water byproduct could cause gas slugs). (4) The 2.0 equiv excess of ethyl nitroacetate may be reducible in flow due to better mixing and heat transfer. (5) Coil reactor ID of 0.75-1.0 mm recommended to balance heat transfer with pressure drop from viscous DES.
